title: Pilot Control Room
subtitle: Operational dashboard for monitoring pilot engagement
select_tenant: Select a tenant to view pilot data
no_pilot_patients: No patients are marked as 'in pilot' for this tenant. Use the enrolment section below to add participants.

immediate_risks.title: Immediate Risks
immediate_risks.subtitle: Patients inactive for 3+ days , requires attention
immediate_risks.empty: No patients at risk , all pilot participants are active.
immediate_risks.info: Patients who have not had any platform activity (recordings, exercises, or completed scenarios) in 3 or more days, or who have never been active. Days Inactive shows how long since their last activity. Use Notify Therapist to alert the assigned therapist about disengaged patients.

missing_feedback.title: Missing Feedback
missing_feedback.subtitle: Exercise submissions without therapist review for 48+ hours
missing_feedback.empty: All submissions have been reviewed , no pending feedback.
missing_feedback.info: Exercise recordings submitted by patients that have been waiting for therapist review for more than 48 hours. Only exercise-related recordings (mini-games, learning path exercises) are tracked here. Waiting time shows hours since submission.

weekly_activity.title: Weekly Activity
weekly_activity.subtitle: All pilot patients , this week's engagement
weekly_activity.info: Weekly engagement status for all pilot patients. A session is a cluster of exercise recordings within 1 hour totaling at least 3 minutes, a completed quiz exercise, or a completed scenario session. Status: Active (2+ sessions), At Risk (1 session), Dropping (0 sessions).

high_performers.title: High Performers
high_performers.subtitle: Patients with 3+ sessions this week
high_performers.info: Patients with 3 or more practice sessions this week. Trend compares this week's session count against last week , positive values indicate improvement, 'new' means this is their first qualifying week.

activity_overview.title: Activity Overview
activity_overview.subtitle: Platform usage across all users in this tenant
activity_overview.no_activity: No activity recorded yet for this tenant.
activity_overview.info: Summary of all platform activity for pilot patients (excluding control group). Tracks audio recordings, completed scenario sessions, and completed exercises. Last Active shows the most recent activity across all types. Practice Time is total recording duration in minutes.

enrollment.title: Pilot Enrolment
enrollment.subtitle: Toggle which users participate in the pilot
enrollment.name: Name
enrollment.control_group_confirm_message: {{name}} will lose access to all app features. They will only see a study enrolment screen after completing their speech assessment.

assessments.title: Speech Assessments
assessments.subtitle: Pre-pilot speech sample collection progress
assessments.request_new_tooltip: Assign new speech recordings to all pilot patients. Patients who already have a pending assessment are skipped.
assessments.confirm_message: This will assign a new speech assessment to every pilot patient who doesn't already have one pending. Patients will need to record two new speech samples (reading + monologue) before they can continue using the platform.

pilot_start.banner_title: Set the pilot start date to unlock analytics
pilot_start.banner_subtitle: Adherence, retention and dropoff charts populate once the pilot has a start date.

errors.load_failed: Failed to load pilot data. Check your connection and try again.
errors.control_group_failed: Failed to update control group status
errors.end_failed: Failed to update pilot end date

actions.notification_sent: Notification logged (placeholder , no email sent yet)

engagement.note: Usage by arm. Averages are per enrolled patient in the arm (inactive patients count as zero), so arms of different sizes stay comparable. See the Outcomes tab for what each group means.

learning_path.title: Learning path
learning_path.info: Progress through the guided learning path and time spent practising, per arm.

exercises.title: Exercises & practice
exercises.info: Exercise completions and self-recorded practice, per arm. Difficulty is the patient's 1-5 self-rating.

scenarios.title: Scenarios
scenarios.info: Conversation scenario sessions, split by mode (guided vs realistic) and source (from a template vs custom), per arm.

mini_games.title: Mini-games
mini_games.info: Mini-game assignments and total completions, per arm.

outcomes.no_data: No data yet for this measure.
outcomes.arm_note: Arms: Intervention ({{intervention}}) = Group 1 + Group 2. Group 1 ({{group1}}) started speech therapy with UpSpeech; Group 2 ({{group2}}) was already in speech therapy. Control ({{control}}) was assessed but did not use the app, so it appears mainly in the pre/post assessment below. On the severity charts, lower is better.

baseline.title: Baseline balance
baseline.info: Onboarding CBSA severity by arm: the check that the arms started comparable. If baseline severity differs a lot between arms, read later outcome gaps with caution. Shown as mean ± SD (0-60, lower is milder).
baseline.note: Large baseline gaps between arms can confound the comparison; small, overlapping ranges support it.

change.title: Self-severity change (first to last)
change.info: Per-arm change in CBSA self-reported severity from each patient's first to last weekly submission. Mean change with 95% CI, effect size (Cohen's d), and how many patients improved, stayed the same, or worsened. Negative change means improvement.
change.note: Only patients with at least two weekly submissions are included. Cohen's d and CI need at least two patients in the arm.

cbsa.title: CBSA self-reported severity
cbsa.info: Mean CBSA severity score (0-60, lower is better) by relative pilot week, per arm. Dense for intervention; the control arm rarely submits weeklies.

therapist.title: Therapist-rated severity
therapist.info: Mean therapist perceived severity (0-10, lower is better) from completed recording reviews, bucketed by the week the recording was made.

disfluency.title: Disfluency rate
disfluency.info: Mean marked disfluency annotations per minute, over recordings carrying at least one disfluency annotation. Lower is better.

prepost.title: Speech assessment: pre vs post
prepost.info: Therapist-rated severity on the first vs latest speech-assessment round, per arm. This is the only outcome the control arm reliably contributes to. n is the number of patients with both rounds scored.
prepost.not_scored: No speech-assessment recordings have been scored yet. Complete reviews of the assessment recordings to populate this.

dose_response.title: Dose-response: practice vs improvement
dose_response.info: Each point is one patient: practice minutes (x) against their CBSA self-severity change (y). A downward cloud, and a negative r, means more practice goes with lower severity, the signal that use drives the change rather than just co-occurring with it. Coloured by arm.
dose_response.no_data: Not enough paired data yet. A point needs both practice time and at least two CBSA submissions.
dose_response.r_caption: Correlation r = {{r}} across {{n}} patients. Negative means more practice goes with lower severity (improvement).
dose_response.r_insufficient: Only {{n}} paired patient(s); at least 3 are needed to estimate a correlation.

signals.note: Untapped pilot data the other tabs don't show: why scenarios ended early, where patients stall, how they rate milestones, how much the AI reports were edited, and what assessment data is still unscored.

endings.title: Scenario early-exit reasons
endings.info: Why scenario sessions ended early, per arm. 'Felt overwhelmed' points to emotional load; 'technical issue' points to product friction.

funnel.title: Learning-path step funnel
funnel.info: Steps started vs finished vs skipped, per arm, with the completed/started ratio. A low ratio flags where patients stall.

milestone.title: Milestone experience ratings
milestone.info: Mean self-rated effort and naturalness (0-9) from milestone questionnaires, per arm, with the number of responses.

report.title: AI report assistance
report.info: How much therapists leaned on the AI report drafts: total reports, the share carrying at least one edit (annotation), average edits per report, and up/down feedback. A low edit rate suggests drafts were accepted with little change.

completeness.title: Assessment data completeness
completeness.info: Where speech assessments and recording reviews sit in the pipeline, and how many assessment recordings are still unscored. Unscored recordings are why the pre/post efficacy view can be empty.
completeness.unscored: {{count}} assessment recording(s) uploaded but not yet scored. Score them in the annotation room to populate the pre/post outcome view.

analytics.pilot_not_started: Set a pilot start date to unlock analytics.

adherence.title: Adherence Threshold
adherence.subtitle: % of users completing ≥2 sessions/week AND ≥6 minutes total
adherence.info: Percentage of pilot patients meeting the weekly adherence target: at least 2 practice sessions AND at least 6 minutes of total recording time per week. A session is a cluster of recordings within 1 hour totaling ≥3 minutes, or a completed quiz exercise. The 60% dashed line marks the minimum acceptable rate. Control group patients are excluded from this metric.

minutes.title: Practice Time per Week
minutes.subtitle: Average and median minutes practised per user per week
minutes.info: Average and median recording practice time per patient per week, in minutes. The median helps identify whether a few heavy users are skewing the average , if the average is much higher than the median, a small group is doing most of the practice. Only exercise-related recordings (mini-games, learning path exercises) count toward practice time.

dropoff.title: Drop-off Curve
dropoff.subtitle: % of users still active each week (survival curve)
dropoff.info: Survival curve showing the percentage of patients still active each week since the pilot started. A patient counts as active if they completed at least 1 practice session that week. A steeper decline indicates faster disengagement. This metric only includes experimental group patients (control group excluded).

dau.title: Daily Active Users
dau.subtitle: Number of users active per day
dau.info: Number of unique patients who recorded an exercise or completed a quiz exercise on each calendar day. Helps identify usage patterns (weekday vs weekend trends) and spot sudden drops in engagement. Each patient is counted only once per day regardless of how many activities they completed.

practices.title: Practices per User
practices.subtitle: Average sessions per user per week
practices.info: Average number of practice sessions per patient per week. A session is defined as a cluster of exercise recordings made within 1 hour that totals at least 3 minutes of audio, or a completed quiz exercise. The target is ≥2 sessions per patient per week to meet the adherence threshold.

dropout.title: Time to Dropout
dropout.subtitle: Week where user first fails adherence or becomes inactive
dropout.info: Histogram showing the distribution of the week where patients first failed to meet adherence and never recovered. A dropout is defined as the first week a patient misses the adherence threshold (≥2 sessions AND ≥6 min) with no subsequent week meeting it. Concentration in early weeks suggests onboarding friction; later dropout may indicate fatigue.

heatmap.title: Adherence Trend
heatmap.subtitle: Weekly adherence per user (green = met threshold, red = missed)
heatmap.info: Per-patient weekly adherence grid. Green cells mean the patient met the threshold (≥2 sessions AND ≥6 minutes of practice) that week; red cells mean they missed it. Reveals patterns like consistent high performers, intermittent users, or patients who disengage after a specific week. Scroll horizontally for more weeks.

severity.title: Self-Reported Severity Change
severity.subtitle: Final minus baseline severity score (CBSA-SR, lower = improvement)
severity.info: Change in self-reported stuttering severity using the CBSA-SR (Communication-Based Self-Assessment of Stuttering , Self-Report) scale. Compares each patient's onboarding baseline score against their most recent weekly assessment. Negative values (green bars) indicate improvement; positive values (red bars) indicate worsening. Patients need both a baseline and at least one follow-up assessment to appear here.

kpi.retention_info: Percentage of pilot patients who are still actively using the platform in week 8. A patient is considered active if they completed at least 1 practice session that week. This KPI is only available once the pilot has been running for 8+ weeks. Control group patients are excluded.
kpi.week1_info: Percentage of pilot patients who completed at least 2 practice sessions during their first week in the program. A session counts when exercise recordings total ≥3 minutes or a quiz exercise is completed. Target: ≥60%. This is a leading indicator , low activation predicts higher dropout.

alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.no_alerts: No alerts , pilot is on track.

snapshots.evolution_subtitle: How key metrics evolved across daily snapshots

past_pilots.empty_subtitle: When you end a pilot, its frozen results are archived here.
past_pilots.load_failed: Failed to load this pilot's report.
past_pilots.title: Past pilots

modal.bullet_archive: This pilot moves to Past pilots (read-only).
modal.bullet_freeze: Metrics freeze to this window; later activity is excluded.
modal.bullet_restart: You can start a new pilot afterwards.
modal.date_hint: Can be a past date to close a pilot you forgot to end.
modal.title: End this pilot?
